elements.text: (a) General eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Stable monthly rental income must be generated from acceptable and verifiable sources and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be reasonably expected to continue for at least the next three years. For each income
elements.label: text

elements.text: source used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must determine that both the source and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the income are stable. Refer to Section 5301.1 for additional information about
elements.label: text

elements.text: income stability and continuance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Rental income eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following property and occupancy types may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered when determining the stable monthly income:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1-unit Primary Residence:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rental income from a live-in aide, regardless of the type of housing provided, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rental income from an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence (rental income is eligible from units that are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupied by the Borrower)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-subject investment property owned by the Borrower (not restricted to residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: property (e.g., commercial permitted))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the Borrower’s second home may not be used as stable
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Rental income generated from ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: When determining stable monthly income, rental income generated from an ADU may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, rental income generated from one or more ADUs on a non-subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: investment property may be considered when determining stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5306.1(g) below for requirements related to rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for a 1-unit Primary Residence with an ADU. Refer to Section 4501.6(b) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements related to rental income eligibility for a 1-unit Primary Residence with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU for Home Possible® Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5601.2 for the property eligibility and appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the subject property with an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5306.2: Automated income assessment using tax data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for rental income (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible and ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other provisions related to rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using tax data provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with the option to use certain Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)) to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties related to the Borrower’s rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s tax data and submit all required data and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the applicable System. Based on the data submitted, the System will assess for
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated using the methods described in Section 5306.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: References in this section to “service provider” mean a third-party service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller obtains the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (Internal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revenue Service (IRS) Form 1040, including Schedule E), the Seller will upload the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: returns to a service provider, which will produce a report (“Income Calculation Report”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: consisting of data extracted from the Borrower’s tax returns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may also request from the IRS, via the service provider, a tax transcript of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s tax return(s). If both tax returns and tax transcripts are obtained, Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor® will compare data from the Borrower’s tax return to data from the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcript for assessment of tax return data representation and warranty relief eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: and return the results of the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, the Seller may request from the IRS, via a service provider, a tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s tax return(s). Loan Product Advisor will retrieve the tax transcript data
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the service provider, assess for income and data representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility and return the results of the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax return or tax transcript data
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may enter data from the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1040 and Schedule E) or tax transcripts into the Freddie Mac Income Calculator,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which will produce a Freddie Mac Income Calculator Certificate that details the results of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Freddie Mac Income Calculator submission, including the calculated income and
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible and ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following property and occupancy types reported on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s IRS Schedule E of the tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) is eligible for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated assessment using tax data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject or non-subject 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence (rental income is eligible from
elements.label: text

elements.text: units not occupied by the Borrower)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-subject investment property owned by the Borrower (not restricted to residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: property (e.g., commercial property permitted))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only rental income reported on the Borrower’s IRS Schedule E is eligible for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated assessment using tax data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 5304 for the treatment of all rental real estate income or loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on IRS Form 8825, Rental Real Estate Income and Expenses of a Partnership or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an S Corporation, which reflects all income and expenses for the rental property, and IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule K-1, which reflects the Borrower’s proportionate share of the net rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: or loss.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following is not eligible for an automated assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: using tax data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ADU on a subject 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Live-in aide residing in a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Automated assessment eligibility for multiple income producing properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income reported on IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax transcript(s) is only
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for an automated assessment when one rental income producing property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on the tax transcript(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income reported on IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return(s) is eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: an automated assessment when one or more rental income producing properties are
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on the tax return(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an
elements.label: text

elements.text: expectation that the rental income will continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Federal income tax return and tax transcript requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) used in the automated income assessment must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (IRS Form 1040, including Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E) that were most recently filed with the IRS. Sellers are encouraged to always confirm
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Borrower that the tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) provided are the most recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed with the IRS. Section 5302.4(b) for the age of tax return requirements apply,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the requirement to obtain IRS Form 4868 when evidence of completed IRS tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing extension is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns must not be any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filed on a fiscal year basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Handwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amended return
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ From a U.S. Territory
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has amended their tax returns, then tax transcripts are not acceptable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment through the applicable System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If using tax transcript(s), the assessment requires the most recent calendar year; a tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is not allowed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Inconsistent or contradictory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for investigating and resolving any inconsistent or contradictory
elements.label: text

elements.text: information contained in the Income Calculation Report; the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator Certificate; Form 92, Net Rental Income Calculations – Schedule E (or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar alternative form); tax returns; tax transcripts; and/or the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller determines that a property from which positive rental income is
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated is no longer owned, the Seller must exclude it from the qualifying income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage receives a tax return data representation and warranty relief result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Not Eligible,” as described in Section 5306.2(e)(iii) below, due to inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the tax transcript returned by the IRS via the service provider and the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return(s) uploaded to the service provider, the Seller is required to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller finds discrepancies, it must update the information in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: System and/or the Income Calculation Report and resubmit the data as necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Mortgages that receive a tax return data representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of “Eligible” as described in Section 5306.2(e)(iii) below, Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor has compared the data from uploaded tax return(s) to data from IRS tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcripts and has found the data to be consistent; therefore, for eligible rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, the Seller is not responsible for investigating inconsistent information
elements.label: text

elements.text: between these documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: When tax returns are obtained and uploaded to a service provider, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Review and verify the Income Calculation Report, if applicable, against information
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file to determine if any adjustments must be made. If so, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must update the Income Calculation Report and upload it to the service provider,
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with any additional documentation necessary to support the updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Submit the most current version of the Income Calculation Report to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor. Loan Product Advisor will assess, on a property level basis, the calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of income and/or losses from the eligible rental income based on data from the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: returns and the Income Calculation Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Income Calculation Report is updated or additional documentation is
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided, the Seller must upload the updated report and/or documentation to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: service provider and submit the updated information to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: When tax transcripts are obtained from a service provider and not used with the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Income Calculator, the Seller must submit the tax transcript data to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor, which assesses, on a property level basis, the calculation of income and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses from the eligible rental income reported on Schedule E based on data from the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcripts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When using tax transcripts, only one rental property reporting on the IRS Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E is allowed. Refer to Section 5306.2(b)(iii) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax data
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller uses the Freddie Mac Income Calculator with tax data, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit accurate tax return data or tax transcript data, as applicable, to the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income Calculator, which will assess, on a property level basis, the calculation of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or losses from the eligible rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When using tax transcripts, only one rental property reporting on the IRS Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E is eligible for an automated assessment. Refer to Section 5306.2(b)(iii) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Representation and warranty relief overview
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: through an automated income assessment using tax data. Eligibility for each type of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following representation and warranty relief is determined separately:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 5306.2: Automated income assessment using tax data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for rental income (Future effective date 06/28/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible and ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other provisions related to rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using tax data provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with the option to use certain Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)) to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties related to the Borrower’s rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s tax data and submit all required data and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the applicable System. Based on the data submitted, the System will assess for
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated using the methods described in Section 5306.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: References in this section to “service provider” mean a third-party service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller obtains the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (Internal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revenue Service (IRS) Form 1040, including Schedule E), the Seller will upload the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: returns to a service provider, which will produce a report (“Income Calculation Report”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: consisting of data extracted from the Borrower’s tax returns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may also request from the IRS, via the service provider, a tax transcript of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s tax return(s). If both tax returns and tax transcripts are obtained, Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor will compare data from the Borrower’s tax return to data from the tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: for assessment of tax return data representation and warranty relief eligibility and return
elements.label: text

elements.text: the results of the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, the Seller may request from the IRS, via a service provider, a tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s tax return(s). Loan Product Advisor® will retrieve the tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: data from the service provider, assess for income and data representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax return or tax transcript data
elements.label: text

elements.text: When data from the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (IRS Form 1040 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule E) or tax transcripts is submitted into the Freddie Mac Income Calculator, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Income Calculator Certificate is produced that details the results of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Income Calculator submission, including the calculated income and
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility for income and data, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible and ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following property and occupancy types reported on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s IRS Schedule E of the tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) is eligible for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated assessment using tax data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject or non-subject 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence (rental income is eligible from
elements.label: text

elements.text: units not occupied by the Borrower)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-subject investment property owned by the Borrower (not restricted to residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: property (e.g., commercial property permitted))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only rental income reported on the Borrower’s IRS Schedule E is eligible for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated assessment using tax data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 5304 for the treatment of all rental real estate income or loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on IRS Form 8825, Rental Real Estate Income and Expenses of a Partnership or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an S Corporation, which reflects all income and expenses for the rental property, and IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule K-1, which reflects the Borrower’s proportionate share of the net rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: or loss.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Ineligible rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following is not eligible for an automated assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: using tax data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ADU on a subject 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Live-in aide residing in a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Automated assessment eligibility for multiple income producing properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income reported on IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax transcript(s) is only
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for an automated assessment when one rental income producing property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on the tax transcript(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income reported on IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return(s) is eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: an automated assessment when one or more rental income producing properties are
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on the tax return(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an
elements.label: text

elements.text: expectation that the rental income will continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Federal income tax return and tax transcript requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) used in the automated income assessment must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s complete federal income tax returns (IRS Form 1040, including Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E) that were most recently filed with the IRS. Sellers are encouraged to always confirm
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Borrower that the tax return(s) or tax transcript(s) provided are the most recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed with the IRS. Section 5302.4(b) for the age of tax return requirements apply,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the requirement to obtain IRS Form 4868 when evidence of completed IRS tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing extension is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns must not be any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filed on a fiscal year basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Handwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amended return
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ From a U.S. Territory
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has amended their tax returns, then tax transcripts are not acceptable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment through the applicable System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If using tax transcript(s), the assessment requires the most recent calendar year; a tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is not allowed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Inconsistent or contradictory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for investigating and resolving any inconsistent or contradictory
elements.label: text

elements.text: information contained in the Income Calculation Report; the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator Certificate; Form 92, Net Rental Income Calculations – Schedule E (or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar alternative form); tax returns; tax transcripts; and/or the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller determines that a property from which positive rental income is
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated is no longer owned, the Seller must exclude it from the qualifying income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage receives a tax return data representation and warranty relief result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Not Eligible,” as described in Section 5306.2(e)(iii) below, due to inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the tax transcript returned by the IRS via the service provider and the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return(s) uploaded to the service provider, the Seller is required to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller finds discrepancies, it must update the information in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: System and/or the Income Calculation Report and resubmit the data as necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Mortgages that receive a tax return data representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of “Eligible” as described in Section 5306.2(e)(iii) below, Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor has compared the data from uploaded tax return(s) to data from IRS tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcripts and has found the data to be consistent; therefore, for eligible rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, the Seller is not responsible for investigating inconsistent information
elements.label: text

elements.text: between these documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: When tax returns are obtained and uploaded to a service provider, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Review and verify the Income Calculation Report, if applicable, against information
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file to determine if any adjustments must be made. If so, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must update the Income Calculation Report and upload it to the service provider,
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with any additional documentation necessary to support the updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Submit the most current version of the Income Calculation Report to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor. Loan Product Advisor will assess, on a property level basis, the calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of income and/or losses from the eligible rental income based on data from the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: returns and the Income Calculation Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Income Calculation Report is updated or additional documentation is
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided, the Seller must upload the updated report and/or documentation to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: service provider and submit the updated information to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: When tax transcripts are obtained from a service provider and not used with the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Income Calculator, the Seller must submit the tax transcript data to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor, which assesses, on a property level basis, the calculation of income and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses from the eligible rental income reported on Schedule E based on data from the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcripts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When using tax transcripts, only one rental property reporting on the IRS Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E is allowed. Refer to Section 5306.2(b)(iii) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax data
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller uses the Freddie Mac Income Calculator with tax data, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit accurate tax return data or tax transcript data, as applicable, to the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income Calculator, which will assess, on a property level basis, the calculation of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or losses from the eligible rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When using tax transcripts, only one rental property reporting on the IRS Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: E is eligible for an automated assessment. Refer to Section 5306.2(b)(iii) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must enter in Loan Product Advisor in the Real Estate/Property You Own
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section of the property information the rental property address as it appears on the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Schedule E of the Borrower’s tax return.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5307: Asset Qualification Sources
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5307.1: Assets as a basis for repayment of obligations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Asset calculation method for establishing the debt payment-to-income (DTI) ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Asset eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets that will be used by the Borrower for the repayment of their monthly obligations may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: used to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage, provided that, regardless of the underwriting path
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage, the requirements of this section and Section 5301.2 are met. Form 65, Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Loan Application, should include information pertaining to the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment and income, even if the Borrower qualifies for the Mortgage solely based on assets.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgage eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The assets described in this Section 5307.1 may only be used to qualify the Borrower if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage meets all of the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is secured by a 1- or 2-unit Primary Residence or a second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is either a purchase transaction Mortgage, “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage has a maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratio of 80%, unless the Mortgage is a Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance Mortgage, in which case the maximum ratios in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4304.3 apply
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Asset calculation method for establishing the DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: To determine the qualifying asset amount used to establish the DTI ratio, the following asset
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation method must be used:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset calculation method
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step Requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 1 Determine the total eligible documented asset amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 2 Subtract all of the following from the total eligible documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Any funds required to be paid by the Borrower to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transaction (e.g., Down Payment and Closing Costs),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Any gift funds and borrowed funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Any portion of assets pledged as collateral for a loan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise encumbered
elements.label: text

elements.text: Net eligible assets: The resulting figure is the “net eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 3 Divide the “net eligible assets” by 240.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Qualifying asset amount: The resulting figure is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “qualifying asset amount” used to establish the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5401: Evaluation of Monthly Obligations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5401.1: Monthly housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages secured by Primary Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages secured by second homes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages with a non-occupying Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental payment documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Housing expense-to-income ratio requirements based on underwriting method
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Guide provisions related to the monthly housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The housing expense-to-income ratio is determined by dividing the Borrower’s monthly housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense by the Borrower’s stable monthly income and/or qualifying asset amount as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5307.1(b). The monthly housing expense must be documented in the Mortgage file and
elements.label: text

elements.text: established as described below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages secured by Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Mortgages secured by a Primary Residence, the following expenses must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the calculation of the monthly housing expense-to-income ratio:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Principal and interest payments on the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Property hazard insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New construction: When the actual real estate tax amount is not yet available, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate tax amount included in the monthly housing expense must be based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value of the improvements and the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of ownership: If the Mortgaged Premises is in a jurisdiction where
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of ownership causes or results in a recalculation of the amount of real
elements.label: text

elements.text: estate tax, the monthly housing expense must include an estimate of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recalculated tax amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tax abatements: The real estate tax amount included in the monthly housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense may be reduced or excluded when the Mortgage file contains all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence to support the reduced real estate tax amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence of continuance for at least five years after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Borrower is not currently receiving the tax abatement, evidence the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and/or the property is eligible for the reduced real estate tax amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tax exemptions: The real estate tax amount may be excluded from the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing expense when the Mortgage file contains all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence to support the reduced real estate tax amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence of continuance for at least five years after the Note Date. However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the tax exemption is due to the Borrower’s age or disability, documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: verifying five years’ continuance is not required, provided the exemption does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not have a predetermined expiration date within five years of the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Borrower is not currently receiving the tax exemption, evidence the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and/or the property is eligible for the tax exemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following additional expenses must be included when applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Flood insurance premiums only if coverage is required in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Leasehold payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Special assessments with more than 10 monthly payments remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Homeowners association dues (excluding unit utility charges)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintenance Fees (excluding unit utility charges)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payments on secondary financing, including a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC).
elements.label: text

elements.text: HELOC payments must be included when there is an outstanding balance on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: account. In the absence of a monthly payment on the credit report for the HELOC,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and if there is no documentation in the Mortgage file indicating a monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount, 1.5% of the outstanding balance is considered the monthly payment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 4204.1 for when documentation of HELOC terms is required and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5501.3 when HELOC proceeds are used for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For properties subject to resale restrictions, recurring monthly payments associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the financial subsidy that was provided when the property was purchased by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. See Section 4406.3 for additional requirements related to such payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages secured by second homes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by second homes and Investment Properties, the monthly housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense is the sum of the monthly charges described above in Section 5401.1(a) for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower does not own but rents their principal domicile, the Borrower’s rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment for that principal domicile must be included in the calculation of the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing expense-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Establishing the monthly housing expense for Mortgages with a non-occupying
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a non-occupying Borrower, the monthly housing expense is the sum of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly charges described above in Section 5401.1(a) for each Borrower’s Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower does not own but rents their principal domicile, the Borrower’s rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment for that principal domicile must be included in the calculation of the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing expense-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Rental payment documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Borrower rents their principal domicile, one of the following is required to verify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly rental payment amount:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Direct verification of rent from a management company
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Direct verification of rent from an individual landlord supported by two months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled checks or other evidence of two months’ payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the current, fully executed lease agreement supported by two months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled checks or other evidence of two months’ payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Six months of canceled checks or bank statements supporting consistent payments in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount used in qualifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Housing expense-to-income ratio requirements based on underwriting method
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages underwritten with Loan Product Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor calculates and assesses the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s qualifying ratios based on submitted data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Accept Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor has determined that the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying ratios are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)For all Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages, the Seller must evaluate the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability to pay the monthly housing expense and other obligations. As a guideline, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly housing expense-to-income ratio should not be greater than 28%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An exception can be made only with an offset documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of offsets that might support the use of higher monthly payment ratios are
elements.label: text

elements.text: found in Section 5401.2(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Generally, more flexibility is appropriate for the monthly housing expense-to-income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio than for the monthly debt payment-to-income ratio. Less flexibility is
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate for situations involving additional layers of risk (i.e., ARMs, a marginal
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation, minimal reserves or maximum financing).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Manually Underwritten Mortgage for which either of the ratio guidelines is
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeded, the Seller must prepare and retain in the Mortgage file a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation justifying its underwriting decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Additional requirements for GreenCHOICE Mortgages® that are Manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For GreenCHOICE Mortgages that are Manually Underwritten Mortgages, higher
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying ratios may be appropriate, considering the impact energy efficiency has on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s utility charges (i.e., an energy-efficient property results in lower
elements.label: text

elements.text: utility charges, allowing the homeowner to apply more income to housing expense).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If one or both of the higher qualifying ratios are used for a GreenCHOICE
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, the Seller must provide one of the following to the appraiser, as well as
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain a copy in the Mortgage file, to evidence that the property has a level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy efficiency greater than that of a “standard” (i.e., non-energy-efficient)
elements.label: text

elements.text: property:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Energy Rating Systems (HERS) report completed by a certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Energy Services Network (RESNET®) Home Energy Rater reflecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: a HERS Index of 90 or below (http://www.resnet.us/directory/search)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Department of Energy (DOE) Home Energy Score Report completed by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: independent Home Energy Score Certified AssessorTM reflecting a DOE Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Energy Score of six or greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: (https://betterbuildingssolutioncenter.energy.gov/home-energy-score/home￾energy-score-map)
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5601.4 for detailed appraisal requirements for properties with energy￾efficient improvements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5401.2: Monthly debt payment-to-income (DTI) ratio
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating debt ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: The DTI ratio is determined by dividing the total of the Borrower’s monthly housing expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5401.1(a) plus all monthly payments on the Borrower’s liabilities described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 5401.2(b) by the Borrower’s stable monthly income and/or qualifying asset amount as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5307.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Consideration of liabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s liabilities must be reflected on the Mortgage application (Form 65,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Residential Loan Application) and considered when qualifying the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must review the Mortgage application, credit report, Borrower’s paystubs (if
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided) and other file documentation for Borrower liabilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of the Borrower’s debts incurred through the Note Date must be considered when
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Debt pay offs and pay downs
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Borrower pays off or pays down an existing debt to qualify for the Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must document the source of funds used. The source of funds must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: asset eligibility and documentation requirements in Sections 5501.3 and 5501.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Liabilities included in the monthly DTI ratio

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documentation verifies that there are 10 or fewer months of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining.

elements.text: included in the Borrower’s DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: if there are more than 10 months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must document in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file that the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not past due under the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: installment agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the amount of taxes owed divided
elements.label: text

elements.text: by 72 must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s monthly DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: There must be no indication, and the Seller must have no knowledge, that the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: has filed a Notice of Federal Tax Lien for the taxes owed by the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Timeshare loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Timeshare loans are considered installment debts, regardless of how they are reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Borrower’s credit report. Associated maintenance fees are not required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the monthly DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Other installment debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: For other installment debts not reported on the credit report, or listed as deferred or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance, the Seller must maintain in the Mortgage file documentation verifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly payment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Alimony or maintenance payments with more than 10 months of payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly payment amount must be documented in the Mortgage file with a copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the signed court order, legally binding separation agreement and/or final divorce decree
elements.label: text

elements.text: or equivalent documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instead of including these payments in the calculation of the debt, they must be deducted
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower’s stable monthly income, and the reduced stable monthly income must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to qualify the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When entering an alimony obligation in Loan Product Advisor, select “Alimony” under
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Income Type” and enter it as a negative number. If the Borrower also receives alimony
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, select “Alimony” under “Income Type” and enter the amount received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage file documentation supports that there are 10 or fewer months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments remaining, the payment may be omitted from the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Child support payments with more than 10 months of payments remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly payment amount must be documented with a copy of the signed court order,
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally binding separation agreement and/or final divorce decree or equivalent
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage file documentation supports that there are 10 or fewer months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments remaining, the payment may be omitted from the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Monthly payments on revolving or 30-day accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Revolving accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is no monthly payment reported on the credit report and no documentation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file indicating the monthly payment amount, 5% of the outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance will be considered the required monthly payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) 30-day accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: For 30-day accounts (i.e., accounts that require the balance to be paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly), the Seller must include the full amount of the outstanding account balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: The debt may be excluded if the Borrower has sufficient funds to pay off
elements.label: text

elements.text: the outstanding account balance. These funds must be verified, in addition to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds used to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage transaction, and the source of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds must be an eligible source as described in Chapter 5501.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Authorized user accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower is not the primary account holder but is an authorized user on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: revolving or 30-day account, the monthly payment, as reported on the credit report,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be included in the DTI ratio only if the Seller is required by Section 5202.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Manually Underwritten Mortgages to include in the Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing that the Borrower has been making the payments on the account for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: last 12 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Monthly lease payments regardless of the number of payments remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a lease is paid down, the monthly lease payment must be included in the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an automobile lease, when the remaining balance is paid off/prepaid, the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease payment may be excluded from the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5401.2(c)(vi) below for requirements when payments for solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a lease agreement, power purchase agreement (PPA) or similar type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement may be excluded from the DTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Monthly payment amounts for properties for which rental income is being
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered for qualification purposes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 5306 for requirements with respect to treatment of debt when using
elements.label: text

elements.text: rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 5304 for requirements with respect to treatment of debt when all rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: income and expenses are reported on IRS Form 8825, Rental Real Estate Income and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Expenses of a Partnership or an S Corporation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Monthly payment amounts for other properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The monthly payment amount must include all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Principal and interest on the First Lien.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information about qualifying rates for the Mortgage secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property, refer to Section 4401.2 for ARM requirements and Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4204.3 for temporary subsidy buydown plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Insurance (e.g., hazard insurance premium and flood insurance premium)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following additional expenses must be included when applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Bridge loan payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Leasehold payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Homeowners association dues (excluding unit utility charges)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Special assessments with more than 10 monthly payments remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintenance Fees (excluding unit utility charges)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payment on any secondary financing (including a Home Equity Line of Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HELOC).
elements.label: text

elements.text: HELOC payments must be included in the monthly DTI ratio when there is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding balance on the account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the absence of a monthly payment on the credit report for the HELOC, and if
elements.label: text

elements.text: there’s no documentation in the Mortgage file indicating a monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount, 1.5% of the outstanding balance will be considered to be the HELOC
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 4204.1 for when documentation of HELOC terms is required and
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Section 5501.3 when HELOC proceeds are used for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Liabilities that may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Contingent liabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: A contingent liability may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio when meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in the table below. The documentation used to exclude the liability must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet the age of documentation requirements in Section 5102.4.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Revolving

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation in the Mortgage file must indicate the
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A party other than the Borrower has been making
elements.label: text

elements.text: timely payments for the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a Mortgage payment is being excluded, the party
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text
