elements.label: text

elements.text: are being made by another party. For example, payments on multiple student loans made
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower’s parent represent a common situation. However, additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigation and documentation might be necessary when a Borrower’s multiple
elements.label: text

elements.text: installment and revolving debts are being paid by the Borrower’s spouse who is not on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Assumed Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio when the Borrower is listed as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower on a Mortgage that has been assumed by another party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has not been legally released from liability on the assumed Mortgage by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer or owner of the Mortgage, the monthly payment may only be excluded from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly DTI ratio when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage file contains documentation of the property transfer, evidencing that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower no longer owns the property, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The assignee (the party who assumed the Mortgage) has made timely payments for at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least the most recent 12 months, as documented by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the fully executed Mortgage assumption agreement, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence of timely payments on the assumed Mortgage for the most recent 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months as documented on the Borrower’s credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Current Primary Residence pending sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s current Primary Residence is pending sale and the sale will not close
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Note Date of the Mortgage or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount for the property pending sale may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file contains an executed sales contract for the property pending sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the executed sales contract includes a financing contingency, the Mortgage file must
elements.label: text

elements.text: also contain evidence that the financing contingency has been cleared or a lender’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitment to the buyer of the property pending sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee relocation programs: For Borrowers being relocated pursuant to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: employee relocation program, the monthly payment amount for the property pending sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio if the Mortgage meets the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4408.1(d)(iii) for Mortgages made pursuant to employee relocation programs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Assigned debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: A liability on a debt, including a Mortgage, may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The obligation to make the payments has been assigned to another by court order,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as a divorce decree, regardless of whether the Borrower is legally released from
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability by the creditor, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller documents the order (e.g., provides appropriate pages from the separation
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or divorce decree) in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Self-employed Borrower’s debt paid by the Borrower’s business
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a self-employed Borrower is obligated on a debt that has been paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s business for 12 months or longer, the monthly payment for the debt may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: excluded from the monthly DTI ratio if the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage file contains evidence that the debt has been paid timely by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s business for no less than the most recent 12 months, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The tax returns evidence that business expenses associated with the debt (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, lease payments, taxes, insurance) have been reported and support that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt has been paid by the business
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Payments for solar panels subject to a lease agreement, PPA or similar type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease payments for solar panels may be excluded from the monthly DTI ratio if the lease:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provides for delivery of a specific amount of energy for an agreed-upon payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: during a given period; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Includes a production guarantee compensating the Borrower on a prorated basis when
elements.label: text

elements.text: the energy produced by the solar panels is less than the level required in the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payments for solar panels subject to a PPA or similar type of agreement may be excluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the monthly DTI ratio if the payment is calculated based only on the generated
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy. A copy of the lease agreement, PPA or similar type of agreement, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be maintained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Payments on installment debts secured by financial assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payments on installment debts secured by financial assets, other than cryptocurrencies, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which repayment may be obtained by liquidating the asset may be excluded from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly DTI ratio when qualifying the Borrower, regardless of the payment amount or
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of payments remaining.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan secured by the financial asset must have been made by a financial institution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may consider only the portion of the funds that exceeds the loan balance as
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds used to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage transaction. See Chapter 5501 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: more information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Evaluating debt ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages underwritten with Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor calculates and assesses the Borrower’s qualifying ratios based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Accept Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor has determined that the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying ratios are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages, the Seller must evaluate the Borrower’s ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay the monthly housing expense and other obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s monthly DTI ratio exceeds 45%, the Mortgage is ineligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As a guideline, the monthly DTI ratio should not be greater than 36%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Borrower’s monthly DTI ratio exceeds 36%, the Seller must document in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file justification for the higher qualifying ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except in rare circumstances, the Borrower’s DTI ratio should not exceed 36% for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-out refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages where there is evidence that the Borrower increases debt and then
elements.label: text

elements.text: periodically uses refinance or debt consolidation loans to reduce payments to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: manageable level
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following factors may be considered in justifying a DTI ratio that exceeds 36% but is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not greater than 45%:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Energy-efficient property: The Mortgage is secured by an energy-efficient property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as described in Section 5401.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Increased earnings probability: The Borrower’s probability for increased earnings
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on education, job training or time employed or practiced in a profession
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rent paid by Related Person: Documented rent paid by Related Persons living in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ability to carry higher expenses: The Borrower demonstrated ability to carry a higher
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing expense or higher debt level while maintaining a good credit history for at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expectation of decreased expenses: The existence of verified income that is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: included within the definition of “stable monthly income” in Section 5301.1 when
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is an expectation that future expenses will be lower (such as child-support
elements.label: text

elements.text: income scheduled to cease in one year when a child becomes an adult with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expectation of either lower future household expenses or additional income provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the new adult)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the examples listed below may be used to justify higher qualifying ratios for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages. However, they may not be used to justify higher
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying ratios for Caution Mortgages because they have already been considered by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Borrower’s verified liquid assets are substantial enough to evidence an ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repay the Mortgage regardless of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. There is a Down Payment on the purchase of the property of at least 25%
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Borrower has a strong Credit Score (for example, a 740 or higher FICO® score),
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller can confirm that the Borrower’s credit reputation is excellent
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Manually Underwritten Mortgage for which either of the ratio guidelines is
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeded, the Seller must prepare and retain in the Mortgage file a written explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: justifying its underwriting decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5103.1 for special ratio requirements when a non-occupying Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: present.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 4302.5 for maximum DTI ratio requirements for Refi Possible® Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5501: Assets
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5501.1: Funds required for the Mortgage transaction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification of Borrower funds for a purchase transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification of Borrower funds for a refinance transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prorated real estate tax credits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluation of deposits in the Borrower’s accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Verification of Borrower funds for a purchase transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transaction Mortgages, the Seller must verify that the Borrower has sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds to qualify for the Mortgage, which includes any funds required to be paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and Borrower reserves. Funds required to be paid by the Borrower include, but are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, funds needed for Down Payment, for Closing Costs and to pay off or pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: down debt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Verification of Borrower funds for a refinance transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: For refinance Mortgages, the Seller must verify that the Borrower has sufficient funds to
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualify for the Mortgage, which includes any funds required to be paid by the Borrower (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds needed for Closing Costs and to pay off or pay down debt) and Borrower reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: when any of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The funds required to be paid by the Borrower are more than $500
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserves are required for the transaction (i.e., as specified in Section 5501.2 or required
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Feedback Certificate)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a Manually Underwritten Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligible sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: All funds required to be verified must come from the eligible sources described in Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5501.6 for interested party contributions requirements and Section 5501.7(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for lender credit requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Prorated real estate tax credits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prorated real estate tax credits contributed by the property seller in areas where real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: taxes are paid in arrears may be considered when determining the funds required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified for the Mortgage transaction only when the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicates that an Escrow account is established and includes the portion of real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: owed by the property seller for the period they owned the property. In such cases, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: prorated tax credit from the property seller offsets that portion of the charge for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: establishment of the Escrow account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) General documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Documentation Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages, the Documentation Level shown on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback Certificate indicates the minimum level of documentation acceptable for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor Mortgage. The Seller must provide the documentation required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this chapter for the Documentation Level returned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Manually Underwritten Mortgages must at a minimum be documented according
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Standard Documentation requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Documentation requirements for Borrower’s accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets held in an account at a financial institution may be verified using account
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements, direct account verifications or third-party asset verifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table contains the requirements for these types of verifications:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Deposits requiring verification for all transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an unverified deposit is used to pay off or pay down an existing debt in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualify for the Mortgage, the source of funds must be documented.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) “Large deposits” requiring verification for purchase transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transaction Mortgages, the Seller must document the source of funds for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any “large deposit” that is needed to qualify the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A “large deposit” is any single deposit exceeding 50% of the sum of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total monthly qualifying income for the Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount derived from the asset calculation for establishing the DTI ratio in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of Section 5307.1 when assets are used as a basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: for repayment of obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a single deposit consists of both verified and unverified portions, the Seller may
elements.label: text

elements.text: use just the unverified amount when determining whether the deposit is a “large deposit.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a “large deposit” is not verified and is not needed to qualify the Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must reduce the funds used for qualification by the amount of the unverified
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposit. For Loan Product Advisor Mortgages, refer to Section 5101.3 to determine when
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmission to Loan Product Advisor is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Acceptable sources of deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a deposit requires verification as stated above, the Seller must determine all of the

elements.label: text

elements.text: The only acceptable sources of deposit are the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reserves are Borrower’s assets remaining after the Mortgage closing. The source of funds used
elements.label: text

elements.text: for reserves, when needed to qualify the Borrower, must meet the eligibility and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 5501.3 and 5501.4.

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Calculation of reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum required reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Calculation of reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reserves are measured by the number of months of the monthly payment amount for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property. The monthly payment amount is the sum of the following monthly charges:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal and interest payments on the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property hazard insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Leasehold payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Homeowners association dues (excluding unit utility charges)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payments on secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When calculating reserves for the subject property, the principal and interest payment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment amount must be based, at a minimum, on the Note Rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When calculating reserves for other properties, the monthly payment amount for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property must be no less than the current monthly payment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Minimum required reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum reserves requirements, as described below, must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) For Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The verified reserves must equal or exceed the following reserves requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Required reserves for Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject property Required reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence – 1-unit None
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Section 4504.7(b) for minimum reserves requirements for HeritageOne®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Multiple Mortgage applications for the same Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller is processing multiple Mortgage applications for the same Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same assets may be used to meet the reserve requirements for each Mortgage transaction.
